overall.description: A gameplay clip from Cyberpunk 2077 showing a character walking along the Northern Pier waterfront. The video begins in first-person perspective along a modern curved building, then transitions to third-person following a character pushing a shopping cart loaded with bedding. The setting features futuristic architecture, palm trees, abandoned stalls, and a cloudy overcast atmosphere.

tall palm trees lining the right side of the pier

segments.scene_description: The clip opens with a first-person perspective walking alongside a sleek curved glass building on a waterfront promenade. A pedestrian walks ahead in the distance, and a roller coaster structure is visible against the overcast sky. The segment ends with a transition to a third-person perspective.

scene_state.functional_zone: Northern Pier open walkway with food vendors and city views

events.description: location text 'Northern Pier' appears on screen, identifying the area

agents.ego_description: character pushing a shopping cart loaded with a mattress and bedding along the pier

entered.detail: shopping cart heavily loaded with a large padded mattress and blankets
illuminated digital menu display showing food items on a concrete planter
elevated highway or monorail structure spanning across the background
tall futuristic buildings with construction cranes visible in the cityscape

segments.scene_description: The perspective shifts to third-person following a character pushing a cart with a mattress along the open pier. The character passes a digital food menu sign and walks toward an overpass bridge, with futuristic skyscrapers and construction cranes visible in the background. The location is identified as 'Northern Pier'.

scene_state.functional_zone: waterfront promenade with abandoned furniture and graffiti walls

agents.ego_description: continuing to push the shopping cart along the waterfront with views of the city skyline

entered.detail: wooden shelving unit left abandoned on the right side of the path
concrete wall covered in graffiti tags and street art
metal railing along the edge of the pier overlooking the water

segments.scene_description: The character continues along the waterfront promenade, passing abandoned furniture and graffiti-covered walls. The camera follows from behind as the cart moves further ahead, with the overpass bridge now visible to the left and futuristic skyscrapers dominating the right side of the frame.

scene_state.functional_zone: abandoned market stalls and covered walkway at pier end

events.description: entering a more enclosed area with covered stalls and overhead structures

agents.ego_description: pushing the cart through a section with abandoned market stalls and shipping containers

entered.detail: abandoned market stall with roof structure and graffiti on the front
large metal shipping container with corrugated sides
scattered chairs and debris along the right side of the walkway

segments.scene_description: The final segment shows the character approaching a more enclosed area with covered market stalls and shipping containers. The camera follows through this semi-covered zone with abandoned furniture scattered about, as the character heads toward a structure at the end of the Northern Pier.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement along a futuristic waterfront promenade that transitions from a sleek architectural vista into a gritty, industrial pier. It begins in a first-person perspective walking beside a curved glass building under an overcast sky, with a distant pedestrian and a roller coaster structure visible ahead. As the perspective shifts to third-person, the camera follows a character pushing a mattress-laden cart past digital food menus and toward a looming overpass bridge, with skyscrapers and construction cranes framing the horizon. The scene then expands across the Northern Pier, passing graffiti-covered walls and abandoned furniture while the urban skyline dominates the right side of the frame. Finally, the camera tracks the character into a more enclosed zone of covered market stalls and shipping containers, moving through scattered debris toward a structure at the end of the pier.
